description: This guide is for content editors using a Hydra-powered site. It covers how to use the editor — how to select things, edit text, add and move blocks, work with containers and templates — without assuming you know how the site was built.
title: Editor Guide

p-1.plaintext: This guide is for **content editors** using a Hydra-powered site. It covers how to use the editor — how to select things, edit text, add and move blocks, work with containers and templates — without assuming you know how the site was built.

children.text: using a Hydra-powered site. It covers how to use the editor — how to select things, edit text, add and move blocks, work with containers and templates — without assuming you know how the site was built.

p-3.plaintext: The editor screen has three regions:

children.text: The editor screen has three regions:

ul-5.plaintext: Toolbar (left) — saving, navigating to other pages, site settings. Standard Volto, plus the Frontend switcher (see below). Live preview (centre) — your actual frontend, running inside an iframe. This is what readers will see. Click directly into the preview to edit. Sidebar (right) — when no block is selected, lists the page-level fields (title, description, blocks). When a block is selected, shows that block's settings, the chain of parent containers, and (for container blocks) the list of children. See Selecting blocks for the navigation patterns.

children.text: — saving, navigating to other pages, site settings. Standard Volto, plus the
— your actual frontend, running inside an iframe. This is what readers will see. Click directly into the preview to edit.

— when no block is selected, lists the page-level fields (title, description, blocks). When a block is selected, shows that block's settings, the chain of parent containers, and (for container blocks) the list of children. See

p-7.plaintext: A toolbar button opens the **Frontend switcher** panel with two sections:

ul-8.plaintext: Viewport — preview the page at common screen sizes (desktop, tablet, mobile). Pure visual switch — no content change. Frontend — list of saved frontend URLs the editor can switch between. Picking one swaps the iframe to that frontend immediately. Same content, different rendering — a Hydra-defining feature: edit a page once, see it on the marketing site, the docs site, the mobile app's web version, and the email-renderer in turn without leaving the page.

children.text: — preview the page at common screen sizes (desktop, tablet, mobile). Pure visual switch — no content change.

— list of saved frontend URLs the editor can switch between. Picking one swaps the iframe to that frontend immediately. Same content, different rendering — a Hydra-defining feature: edit a page once, see it on the marketing site, the docs site, the mobile app's web version, and the email-renderer in turn without leaving the page.

p-9.plaintext: A **Settings** button at the bottom of the panel manages the saved URLs (add, remove, rename). The currently active frontend is highlighted in the list.

children.text: button at the bottom of the panel manages the saved URLs (add, remove, rename). The currently active frontend is highlighted in the list.

img-10.alt: Frontend switcher panel — Viewport section with Mobile/Tablet/Desktop, Frontend section listing four saved frontends, Settings button at the bottom.

h-11.plaintext: Two ways to edit any field

children.text: Two ways to edit any field

p-12.plaintext: Most fields can be edited from either side:

children.text: Most fields can be edited from either side:

ul-13.plaintext: From the preview — click the rendered text/image/link directly and start typing or replacing media. From the sidebar — find the field in the block's settings panel and edit it there.

children.text: — click the rendered text/image/link directly and start typing or replacing media.
— find the field in the block's settings panel and edit it there.

p-14.plaintext: Sidebar editing is always available. Inline editing depends on the frontend supporting it for that field; some fields show a thin underline when hovered to signal they're inline-editable. Either way the result is the same — there's only one source of truth.

children.text: Sidebar editing is always available. Inline editing depends on the frontend supporting it for that field; some fields show a thin underline when hovered to signal they're inline-editable. Either way the result is the same — there's only one source of truth.

p-16.plaintext: A block is a discrete piece of page content with a type (slate, image, listing, slider, etc.), a schema (its fields), and a position. Blocks can be added, removed, moved, and configured. Some blocks contain other blocks — those are called **container blocks** (columns, accordion, slider, grids, sections).

children.text: A block is a discrete piece of page content with a type (slate, image, listing, slider, etc.), a schema (its fields), and a position. Blocks can be added, removed, moved, and configured. Some blocks contain other blocks — those are called

p-17.plaintext: The page itself is a list of blocks (sometimes split across multiple regions like header / content / footer). When you click into the preview, you're clicking on a block.

children.text: The page itself is a list of blocks (sometimes split across multiple regions like header / content / footer). When you click into the preview, you're clicking on a block.

p-19.plaintext: Pressing `Escape` is always safe. It progressively backs out:

children.text: is always safe. It progressively backs out:

ol-20.plaintext: If you're typing in a text field → leaves text editing, the block stays selected. If a block is selected (block mode) → goes up to the parent container. If nothing is selected → no-op.

children.text: If you're typing in a text field → leaves text editing, the block stays selected.
If a block is selected (block mode) → goes up to the parent container.

p-21.plaintext: So `Escape` repeatedly takes you up one level at a time. See [Selecting blocks](selecting-blocks.md) for what selection looks like at each level.
